request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We need to output JSON with one verdict per candidate_id, indicating if the item is valid ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge. Accept only if it is useful, grounded operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence. Allowed category_if_not values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

We must check each candidate. Candidates are given with candidate_id, knowledge_type, title, section_id, summary, evidence_quote, source_pages.

We need to decide if each is valid. Likely all are valid? But let's check each one against the criteria.

cand_04b654061c1d15c6: "Control Loop Enable Based on System Status" - This is from Section 5.1.2, evident quote: "Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup." This is a clear operational rule. Valid.

cand_f0d294b53f23f997: "Control Loop Initialization to Neutral" - Section 5.1.3, quote: "When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents (such as the proportional and integral terms) shall be set initially to a neutral value." Valid.

cand_efe75c5774617d54: "Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Validity at AHU Intake" - Section 5.1.5.1, quote: "Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled." Valid.

cand_bf0952ec594fef2b: "Global Outdoor Air Temperature Calculation" - Section 5.1.5.2, quote: "The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings." Valid.

cand_d8e9a664335e9ddf: "Control Loop Output Rate-of-Change Limit" - Section 5.1.9, quote: "To avoid abrupt changes in equipment operation, the output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute." Valid.

cand_031cdcfeba03deb7: "Alarm Levels Definition" - Section 5.1.12.1, quote: "There shall be 4 levels of alarm a. Level 1: Life-safety message b. Level 2: Critical equipment message c. Level 3: Urgent message d. Level 4: Normal message" - This is a definition, but it is operational and used in sequences. It's not generic; it defines the alarm levels for Guideline 36. Valid.

cand_6a59972e2f91eba5: "Alarm Exit Hysteresis Defaults" - Section 5.1.12.3, but the evidence_quote is just the section heading: "5.1.12.3.  Exit Hysteresis". The summary says "Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default 5 seconds) and an adjustable percent-of-limit-based hysteresis (default 0%)." The evidence quote is insufficient to support the summary; it's only the heading. That might be considered unsupported because the actual content is missing. However, the candidate provides a summary that likely came from that section. We need to judge if the extracted knowledge is valid given the evidence. The evidence quote is minimal, but it does indicate the section where the information can be found. In the context of validating extractions, if the evidence quote is just a section heading, it might be considered insufficient to confirm the summary. But the instruction says: "Accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject ... summaries not supported by evidence." Here, the evidence does not support the summary because the quote is just the heading. So we should reject as unsupported. category_if_not should be "unsupported". But wait: maybe they expect that the summary is derived from the section, and the heading is enough to indicate the source. However, the prompt says "evidence_quote" should support the summary. The quote "5.1.12.3. Exit Hysteresis" does not contain the content about defaults. So it's unsupported. I'll mark it as invalid with category "unsupported".

cand_71d40bdfa4ea1f7d: "Alarm Latching Defaults" - Section 5.1.12.4, evidence quote: "5.1.12.4.  Latching . A latching alarm requires acknowledgment  from the operators before it can return to" - This is also incomplete; the summary says "Default latching status is: Level 1 latching, Level 2 latching, Level 3 nonlatching, Level 4 nonlatching." The evidence quote is only the heading and a partial sentence, not the defaults. So again unsupported. Reject.

cand_9d3fe450d58f1ee8: "Post-Exit Alarm Suppression Periods" - Section 5.1.12.5, evidence quote: "5.1.12.5.  Post-exit Suppression Period.  To limit ala rms, any alarm may have an adjustable suppression" - Again incomplete, summary has specific default periods. Unsupported. Reject.

cand_e1c2939e10fb6737: "VFD Speed Output Configuration" - Section 5.1.13.1, quote: "The speed AO sent to VFDs shall be configured such that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% speed corresponds to maximum speed configured in the VFD." This is a full quote that matches the summary. Valid.

cand_e176371206fea384: "VFD Minimum Speed Mismatch Detection" - Section 5.1.13.2, quote: "5.1.13.2. For each piece o f equipment, the minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point; in the" - This is incomplete, summary says: "Every 60 minutes, read the VFD's active minimum speed via network. If it differs from the software-point minimum speed, write the software minimum speed back to the VFD and generate a Level 4 alarm." The evidence quote doesn't contain that. Unsupported. Reject.

cand_8901bed0cd2f0325: "Rogue Zone Detection via T&R Request Hours" - Section 5.1.14.2.a.6, quote: "A Level 4 alarm is generated if the zone Importance-Multiplier is greater than zero, the zone/system Cumulative% Request Hours exceeds 70%, and the total number of zone/system run hours exceeds 40." This is a clear quote. Valid.

cand_93570bd72e74889b: "Parallel Equipment Rotation Requirement" - Section 5.1.15.1, quote: "Parallel equipment shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear." Valid.

cand_414e69595b7306d1: "Lead/Lag Equipment Rotation" - Section 5.1.15.3, quote: "Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment." Valid.

cand_c5547ae2443ef217: "Lead/Standby Rotation When Equipment Off" - Section 5.1.15.4.a, quote: "Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment." Valid.

cand_8bd742f752455ad3: "Lead/Standby Rotation for Continuously Running Equipment" - Section 5.1.15.4.b, quote: "If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off." Valid.

cand_d808006b292fb536: "Fan/Pump Fault Detection" - Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i.a, quote: "Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on." Valid.

cand_362f9f67d17c6748: "Chiller Fault Conditions" - Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.ii, quote: "5.1.15.5.b.1.ii  or a chilled water or condenser water pump  dedicated to that chiller is faulted per" - This is incomplete; the summary lists specific conditions. Unsupported. Reject.

cand_ef87a102ba424354: "Boiler Fault Conditions" - Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.iii, quote: "5.1.15.5.b.1.iii  or a hot water pump dedicated to that boil er is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i ;" - Incomplete. Unsupported. Reject.

cand_5bc2cb0462e90cb6: "Cooling Tower Fault Conditions" - Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.iv, quote: "iv. Cooling Towers (a) Tower fan has failed as defined above, or Retain the following sentence for cooling towers with inlet isolation valve position feedback. Delete otherwise. (b) Inlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open, or Retain the following sentence for cooling towers with outlet isolation valve position feedback. Delete otherwise. (c) Outlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open." This includes the conditions. Valid.

cand_c5193e35074a209b: "ASHRAE 90.1 Differential Dry Bulb Economizer High Limit" - Section 5.1.17.2, quote: "Differential dry bulb  1b, 2b, 3b, 3c, 4b, 4c,5a, 5b, 5c, 6a, 6b, 7, 8  TOA > TRA" - This is a bit cryptic but seems to be from a table. It supports the summary: "When using differential dry bulb high limit, disable economizer when outdoor air temperature exceeds return air temperature." Valid, as it's an operational rule.

cand_800073c25b9f958c: "Title 24 Differential Dry Bulb Economizer High Limit (California Zones 1,3,5,11-16)" - Section 5.1.17.3, quote: "Differential dry bulb  1, 3, 5, 11  to 16 TOA > TRA" - Similar to above, valid.

cand_7b8031a5f08ea865: "SystemOK TRUE Conditions" - Section 5.1.19.3, quote: "SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load" - Valid.

cand_19b990b9ebc71d2e: "SystemOK FALSE Conditions" - Section 5.1.19.4, quote: "5.1.19.4.  SystemOK shall be FALSE  while the system is starting up (i.e., before reaching setpoint ) or when" - This is incomplete; the summary includes details about component alarms and operator overrides. Unsupported. Reject.

cand_782a3e97e4eabf5a: "Hierarchical Alarm Suppression Rules" - Section 5.1.19.5, quote: "56 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.1.19.5.  The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database)" - This is incomplete; summary lists specific suppression rules. Unsupported. Reject.

Now, for the rejected ones, we need to assign a category_if_not. The options: front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other. For those with insufficient evidence, "unsupported" is appropriate.

Candidate cand_6a59972e2f91eba5: evidence quote just section heading, summary unsupported -> unsupported.
cand_71d40bdfa4ea1f7d: similar -> unsupported.
cand_9d3fe450d58f1ee8: unsupported.
cand_e176371206fea384: unsupported.
cand_362f9f67d17c6748: unsupported.
cand_ef87a102ba424354: unsupported.
cand_19b990b9ebc71d2e: unsupported.
cand_782a3e97e4eabf5a: unsupported.

All others are valid.

So verdicts: 21 candidates, 13 valid, 8 invalid with category "unsupported".

Now output JSON.
